query: California gubernatorial primary polling schedule May 2026 Emerson Berkeley IGS PPIC CBS YouGov upcoming polls
answer:
# California 2026 Gubernatorial Primary: Polling Landscape (May 2026)

## Election Overview

California's **June 2, 2026 nonpartisan top-two primary** features 61 certified candidates. Under this system, all candidates regardless of party appear on one ballot, and the top two vote-getters advance to the November 3 general election. The fragmented Democratic field has raised alarm that **two Republicans could advance**, effectively locking Democrats out of the general election.

---

## Current Polling Averages (Cross-Aggregator, as of May 4-6, 2026)

| Candidate | Party | Avg. % |
|---|---|---|
| **Steve Hilton** | R | ~19.0% |
| Xavier Becerra | D | ~14.7% |
| Tom Steyer | D | ~15.4% |
| Chad Bianco | R | ~13.2% |
| Katie Porter | D | ~9.0% |
| Matt Mahan | D | ~6.0% |
| Antonio Villaraigosa | D | ~3.3% |

Aggregators consulted: 270toWin, Decision Desk HQ, Race to the WH, RealClearPolitics, FiftyPlusOne.

---

## Polls from Specified Pollsters

### Emerson College (6 polls found; sponsor: Inside California Politics)
- **Most recent: April 14–15, 2026** (1,000 LV, ±3.0%): Hilton 17%, Bianco 14%, Steyer 14%, Becerra 10%, Porter 10%
- Prior polls: March 7–9, Feb 13–14, Dec 1–2 2025, Oct 20–21 2025, April 12–14 2025
- **Cadence:** Roughly monthly — a May 2026 edition is expected but not yet released.

### Berkeley IGS / LA Times (3 polls found)
- **Most recent: March 9–14, 2026** (3,889 LV, ±2.5%): Hilton 17%, Bianco 16%, Swalwell 13%, Porter 13%, Steyer 10%
- Prior polls: Oct 20–27 2025, Aug 11–17 2025
- **Cadence:** Roughly quarterly — a late-May or early-June release is plausible but not confirmed.

### PPIC — Public Policy Institute of California (3 polls found)
- **Most recent: March 26 – April 3, 2026** (1,008 LV, ±3.9%): Swalwell 18%, Hilton 17%, Bianco 14%, Steyer 14%, Porter 10%
- Prior polls: Feb 3–11 2026, Nov 13–19 2025
- **Cadence:** Monthly statewide surveys. A May 2026 release is expected (PPIC typically publishes mid-month).

### CBS News / YouGov (1 poll found)
- **April 23–27, 2026** (1,479 LV, ±4.1%): Hilton 16%, Steyer 15%, Becerra 13%, Bianco 10%, Porter 9%, Undecided 26%
- **Cadence:** CBS/YouGov polls irregularly on state races; another pre-primary poll is possible but unconfirmed.

---

## Other Recent Polls (Most Recent First)

| Pollster | Dates | N | Leader | 2nd Place |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Evitarus (D)/CA Dem Party | Apr 30–May 2 | 1,200 LV | Becerra 18% | Hilton 18% |
| SurveyUSA | Apr 28–May 1 | 991 LV | Hilton 20% | Steyer 18% |
| Gudelunas Strategies | Apr 23–27 | 800 LV | Becerra 24% | Hilton 23% |
| EMC Research (D) | Apr 21–26 | 1,000 LV | Becerra 21% | Hilton 20% |
| IVN/Independent Voter Project | Apr 14–20 | 3,404 LV | Becerra 23% | Hilton 20% |
| Kreate Strategies | Apr 12–18 | 900 LV | Hilton 18% | Steyer 16% |

---

## Key Race Dynamics

1. **Steve Hilton (R) is the frontrunner** across all aggregators (~19%). His support was boosted by Trump's endorsement on April 10, 2026.

2. **The critical "second slot" battle** is between Becerra and Steyer (both Democrats), with Bianco (R) close behind at ~13%. If a Republican and a Democrat take the top two, it's a competitive general. If two Republicans advance, Democrats are locked out.

3. **Eric Swalwell's collapse** (suspended April 12 after sexual assault allegations) reshuffled the race. His 13–18% support appears to have migrated primarily to Becerra and Steyer.

4. **Katie Porter's decline** — from 21% (PPIC Dec 2025) to ~9% currently — has been a major shift.

5. **Undecided voters remain very high** (14–26% depending on pollster), leaving significant volatility heading into the June 2 primary.

6. **Democratic fragmentation** is the defining strategic issue: 6+ Democrats splitting ~48% of the vote while 2 Republicans consolidate ~32%.

---

## Upcoming Polls Expected in May 2026

No specific release dates have been publicly announced, but based on historical cadence:
- **Emerson College**: Has polled roughly monthly; a May edition is expected (likely mid-May)
- **PPIC**: Publishes monthly statewide surveys, typically mid-month; a May release is highly likely
- **Berkeley IGS/LA Times**: A pre-primary poll is plausible but not confirmed (last polled in March)
- **CBS/YouGov**: May release another poll closer to the June 2 primary
- **SurveyUSA**: Has been polling frequently; another late-May poll is likely
- **Evitarus/CA Dem Party**: Running tracking polls every ~2 weeks; next expected mid-May
- **IVN**: Has been polling monthly; another release expected

---

key_facts: California's nonpartisan top-two primary is scheduled for June 2, 2026, with 61 certified candidates.
Steve Hilton (R) leads all polling aggregators at approximately 19%, boosted by Trump's endorsement on April 10, 2026.
The second-place battle is between Xavier Becerra (D) at ~14.7% and Tom Steyer (D) at ~15.4%, with Chad Bianco (R) close behind at ~13.2%.
Eric Swalwell (D) suspended his campaign April 12, 2026 following sexual assault allegations; he had been polling 13-18%.
Katie Porter (D) has declined from 21% (PPIC Dec 2025) to approximately 9% in current averages.
Undecided voters remain substantial at 14-26% across polls, leaving significant volatility.
Democratic vote fragmentation across 6+ candidates (~48% combined) vs. Republican consolidation between 2 candidates (~32%) creates risk of two Republicans advancing.
Emerson College has conducted 6 polls in this race (most recent: April 14-15, 2026); roughly monthly cadence suggests a May release is expected.
Berkeley IGS last polled March 9-14, 2026 (3,889 LV); a pre-primary poll is plausible but unconfirmed.
PPIC last polled March 26-April 3, 2026 (1,008 LV); monthly cadence makes a May release highly likely.
CBS/YouGov polled April 23-27, 2026 (1,479 LV); another pre-primary poll is possible but unconfirmed.
No specific upcoming poll release dates from any of these pollsters have been publicly announced as of May 9, 2026.
The most recent poll is the Evitarus/CA Dem Party tracker from April 30-May 2, 2026 showing Becerra and Hilton tied at 18%.
Betty Yee withdrew April 20, 2026 and endorsed Steyer; Toni Atkins dropped out September 29, 2025 and also endorsed Steyer.
